title: Separating the Protein of Interest from MBP after TEV Protease Cleavage (NEB #E8201)
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"415j4","text":"Please note that NEBExpress Ni Resin is chemically resistant to DTT. If you are using a different resin that is incompatible with DTT, you will need to buffer exchange or modify the TEV Protease reaction conditions accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":227}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d7i3o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7c8il","text":"Purchase or prepare the following buffer: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3auv2","text":"Binding Buffer: 1X TEV Protease Reaction Buffer (or similar):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":61}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"an8gk","text":"50 mM Tris-HCl, 0.5 mM EDTA, 1 mM DTT, pH 7.5","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5pi41","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Gently shake the Ni Resin bottle to completely resuspend the slurry and transfer desired amount to a closed column; allow resin to settle.
Open column and allow storage buffer to flow through.
Equilibrate the resin by adding 5 column volumes (CV) of Binding buffer.

Load the TEV Protease digestion directly onto the equilibrated column and allow it to flow through the resin slowly (flow rate < 1 ml/minute). This provides sufficient time for the MBP-tag, TEV Protease, and any remaining MBP-fusion protein to effectively bind to the Ni resin.
Collect the flow through containing the protein of interest in a sterile tube.
Wash the column with 1 CV of Binding Buffer to maximize the yield of the target protein; collect the flow through.
Dialyze or buffer exchange target protein into desired storage buffer.
Add 1 Molarity (M), NaOH to the column and allow a contact time of 01:00:00 to 02:00:00 .
Add 1.5 Molarity (M), NaCl to the column and allow a contact time of 00:10:00 – 00:15:00 followed by 10 CV of water.
For long-term storage, NEBExpress Ni Resin should be stored in 20% ethanol at 2 – 8°C.
creator: New England   Biolabs
